CAT01-020.headline: De-Escalating Overwhelming Emotions
CAT01-020.subtitle: A step-by-step guide for calming intensity

CAT01-020.sceneLine: A tall surging wave of teal-accent forms eases downward into a low, settled pale teal band beside the figure, suggesting a peak of feeling guided back to calm.

CAT01-037.headline: Emotional Contagion
CAT01-037.subtitle: How other people's moods affect you
CAT01-037.figureLine: A single standing clay figure leans subtly as a faint pull crosses its torso, posture absorbing an outside current, conveying another's mood quietly seeping in.
CAT01-037.sceneLine: Soft light teal ripples spread outward from an unseen point and lap against the figure's edges, suggesting feeling that travels invisibly between people.

CAT01-054.headline: The Gut-Brain Connection
CAT01-054.subtitle: How the gut shapes everyday mood
CAT01-054.figureLine: A single seated clay figure rests one hand low on its midsection and the other near its head, posture attentive and connected, conveying two centers in quiet dialogue.
CAT01-054.sceneLine: A continuous teal-accent thread loops gently between the figure's lower body and upper form, suggesting a steady two-way conversation along an unseen line.

CAT01-074.headline: Positive Emotions Build Resilience
CAT01-074.subtitle: How good feelings strengthen the mind
CAT01-074.figureLine: A single standing clay figure widens its stance and lifts its chest slightly, posture rooted and open, conveying inner steadiness being gradually built.

CAT02-002.headline: Normal Worry vs Anxiety Disorder

CAT02-002.sceneLine: Two teal-accent forms sit side by side near the figure, one small and contained, one larger and spreading, suggesting ordinary worry beside something heavier.

CAT02-003.headline: How Avoidance Fuels Anxiety
CAT02-003.subtitle: Why stepping back keeps the cycle going

CAT02-003.sceneLine: A teal-accent ring loops back on itself in a closed circuit beside the figure, soft light teal where it tightens, suggesting a cycle that keeps returning.

CAT02-004.headline: Why Anxiety Feels Physical

CAT02-004.sceneLine: Fine teal-accent lines radiate outward through the figure's form into soft light teal tremors, suggesting an inner alarm felt physically throughout the body.

CAT02-005.sceneLine: A small family of teal-accent forms, each a different size and contour, sits in a gentle arc beside the figure, suggesting related but distinct shapes of worry.

CAT02-006.figureLine: A single seated clay figure rests with hands cupped together as if holding several small things at once, posture contemplative, conveying many roots considered together.
CAT02-006.sceneLine: Three soft light teal strands rise from separate points and converge into one teal-accent form near the figure, suggesting several causes meeting in one place.

CAT02-007.headline: How Anxiety Becomes Chronic

CAT02-007.sceneLine: A teal-accent groove deepens gradually across the ground beside the figure, growing more defined toward soft light teal, suggesting a pattern carved over time.

CAT02-008.headline: Global Anxiety Prevalence

CAT02-010.headline: Anxiety and Excitement

CAT02-010.sceneLine: A single bright teal-accent surge rises beside the figure, ambiguous in direction, fading to soft light teal, suggesting one energy felt as two feelings.

CAT02-011.headline: Panic Attacks Explained

CAT02-011.sceneLine: A concentrated teal-accent burst expands sharply then loosens into soft light teal at its edges beside the figure, suggesting a peak that rises fast and eases.

CAT02-013.headline: Hyperventilation and Anxiety

CAT02-015.headline: Health Anxiety

CAT02-016.headline: Anxiety and Digestion

CAT02-017.headline: Panic Disorder

CAT02-017.sceneLine: Several teal-accent bursts repeat at uneven intervals along a soft light teal line beside the figure, suggesting episodes that keep recurring over time.

CAT02-018.headline: Interoception and Anxiety

CAT02-019.headline: Agoraphobia

CAT02-020.headline: Nocturnal Panic Attacks

CAT02-020.sceneLine: A single teal-accent ripple disturbs an otherwise still, dark soft light teal pool beside the figure, suggesting calm rest interrupted by a quiet wave.

CAT02-022.headline: The Inner Critic

CAT02-022.figureLine: A single seated clay figure inclines its head as if toward a small, insistent presence at its shoulder, posture braced against quiet judgment, conveying a harsh inner voice.
CAT02-022.sceneLine: A small dense teal-accent form hovers close to the figure's shoulder casting a long soft light teal shadow, suggesting a critical voice looming larger than its size.

CAT02-023.headline: Performance Anxiety

CAT02-023.figureLine: A single standing clay figure pauses at the edge of an open space, weight gathered as if before a step into view, posture poised between holding back and beginning.
CAT02-023.sceneLine: A wide teal-accent pool of light spreads in the open space before the figure, soft light teal at its rim, suggesting a moment of being watched, met head-on.

CAT02-025.headline: Social Media and Social Anxiety
CAT02-025.subtitle: How feeds amplify fear of judgment

CAT02-025.sceneLine: Countless tiny soft light teal forms swarm and multiply around the figure's head, teal-accent where they cluster densest, suggesting endless glances amplified.

CAT02-026.headline: Dating with Social Anxiety

CAT02-026.sceneLine: A soft light teal bridge of stepping forms reaches gently across a gap beside the figure toward a calm pale teal glow, suggesting connection approached one step at a time.

CAT02-027.headline: Avoidance in Social Anxiety

CAT02-027.figureLine: A single standing clay figure angles its head away from a softly glowing point, one hand half-raised as if to shield, posture flinching from direct contact.

CAT02-028.headline: Selective Mutism in Adults
CAT02-028.subtitle: When anxiety silences speech

CAT02-028.sceneLine: A teal-accent form rises toward the figure's mouth then stops short, dissolving into soft light teal mist, suggesting speech that gathers but cannot emerge.

CAT02-031.headline: What Chronic Stress Does

CAT02-031.figureLine: A single standing clay figure bears a steady downward weight on its shoulders yet keeps its feet planted, posture enduring, conveying long pressure carried over time.
CAT02-031.sceneLine: A broad soft light teal weight rests across the figure's shoulders, teal-accent where it presses, suggesting an ongoing load held day after day.

CAT02-034.headline: Decision Fatigue

CAT02-034.sceneLine: Many small teal-accent forms crowd around the figure, dimming one by one to pale teal, suggesting available energy thinning with each choice made.

CAT02-035.headline: Stress and Memory

CAT02-035.sceneLine: A teal-accent form begins to scatter into loose soft light teal fragments as the figure reaches, suggesting clear thinking coming apart under strain.

CAT02-036.headline: How Stress Harms the Body
CAT02-036.subtitle: The physical toll built up over time
CAT02-036.figureLine: A single standing clay figure leans under a persistent weight, its surface faintly worn, posture weathered but upright, conveying long strain showing in the body.
CAT02-036.sceneLine: Fine teal-accent stress lines spread slowly across a broad pale teal form beside the figure, suggesting steady pressure leaving its mark over years.

CAT02-037.headline: Financial Stress

CAT02-037.sceneLine: A teal-accent loop tightens repeatedly around a small space beside the figure before one strand loosens into soft light teal, suggesting a worry cycle starting to ease.

CAT02-038.headline: Caregiver Stress and Burnout
CAT02-038.subtitle: When caring for others depletes everything

CAT02-039.headline: Allostatic Load
CAT02-039.subtitle: How stress quietly accumulates

CAT02-039.sceneLine: Small teal-accent forms settle one atop another into a tall soft light teal column beside the figure, suggesting stress quietly accumulating layer by layer.

CAT02-040.headline: Stress Inoculation

CAT02-040.sceneLine: A series of small teal-accent waves meet the figure, each absorbed more easily into pale teal calm, suggesting controlled doses building lasting steadiness.

CAT02-051.headline: What Are Phobias?

CAT02-051.sceneLine: A small teal-accent form casts an enormous soft light teal shadow toward the figure, suggesting a modest object made overwhelming by fear.

CAT02-052.headline: Common Phobias Explained

CAT02-052.sceneLine: A soft light teal ground falls away into a deep teal-accent void just beyond the figure's feet, suggesting a common fear of height held at a careful distance.

CAT02-053.headline: How Phobias Develop

CAT02-053.figureLine: A single standing clay figure tenses at a neutral shape as a faint link forms between it and a remembered jolt, posture newly wary, conveying a fear being learned.
CAT02-053.sceneLine: A thin teal-accent thread ties a plain pale teal form to a sharp soft light teal spark beside the figure, suggesting a harmless thing bound to alarm.

CAT02-055.headline: Needle Phobia

CAT02-055.sceneLine: A single thin teal-accent line approaches the figure, which leans into surrounding soft light teal calm, suggesting a small feared point met with steadiness.

CAT02-057.headline: Animal Phobias

CAT02-058.headline: Emetophobia

CAT02-059.headline: Blood-Injection-Injury Phobia

CAT02-062.headline: The 5-4-3-2-1 Technique

CAT02-063.headline: Progressive Muscle Relaxation

CAT02-063.figureLine: A single reclining clay figure releases its limbs in sequence, tension visibly leaving each one, posture unwinding part by part, conveying the body letting go in stages.
CAT02-063.sceneLine: A series of taut teal-accent forms loosen one after another into soft, slack soft light teal shapes along the figure, suggesting tension released muscle by muscle.

CAT02-064.sceneLine: A teal-accent line traces a smooth, even square path beside the figure, soft light teal at each turn, suggesting breath paced into a steady, balanced cycle.

CAT02-065.sceneLine: A knotted teal-accent form beside the figure slowly unwinds into a clear, ordered pale teal line, suggesting an anxious thought untangled and reshaped.

CAT02-066.headline: Worry Time

CAT02-067.headline: Behavioral Activation

CAT02-069.headline: Cold Water Therapy

CAT02-069.sceneLine: A crisp teal-accent ripple meets the figure and settles into a wide, calming pale teal stillness, suggesting a cool shock that quiets the body's alarm.

CAT02-070.headline: Personalized Anxiety Management

CAT02-071.headline: Anxiety and Relationships
